summary: The correct answer must reflect an item falsely claiming to denote maturation where it doesn't—so only choice A contains such misinformation because stating 'lethitin/sphingomyelin >2' shows immaturity violates established criteria (it should signal maturity). Therefore, even though all indicators relate correctly to maturity under proper understanding, A erroneously frames them as indicating immaturity due to flawed logic or wording—an inconsistency making it the EXCEPTION.
